label: DCM-SchemaRegistry
info:
Backend flows for Schema Registry Management.

════════════════════════════════════════════════
  HOW TO SET YOUR OPENAI API KEY
════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Double-click this flow tab name (DCM-SchemaRegistry)
2. In the Edit Flow panel, click the 'Environment Variables' section
3. Find the variable named: OPENAI_API_KEY
4. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real OpenAI API key
   (e.g. sk-proj-abc123...)
5. Click Done, then Deploy

All OpenAI request nodes in this flow use this single key.
Do NOT paste the key anywhere else.

════════════════════════════════════════════════

OpenAI calls use real http request nodes — not simulated.

MongoDB collections used:
  sr_prompts     – prompt records
  sr_test_cases  – saved test cases

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin.

name: ══ SETUP: Double-click the flow tab → set OPENAI_API_KEY env var ══
info:
WHERE TO PUT YOUR OPENAI API KEY:

1. Double-click the tab name 'DCM-SchemaRegistry' at the top
2. Open 'Environment Variables'
3. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real key
4. Click Done → Deploy

All OpenAI-dependent operations (enhance prompt, code generation)
use real http request nodes that POST to
https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions.

Function nodes are used ONLY for:
  – payload shaping / request preparation
  – response parsing
  – validation and status updates
  – MongoDB query preparation
  – dry-run code execution
